content:
Character: Anna Kowalski
Perception: Anna found her way to the rooftop through Katie last fall, and the first thing I noticed was that she brought a notebook and didn't seem to need anyone to be entertaining. That's an underrated quality. She's a journalism student working on a thesis about Polish immigrant construction workers — which is also, I think, about her father, though she hasn't said that. There's something in how she listens — precise and patient — that makes sense when you know she grew up translating for her parents, where the wrong word could cost her family something real. We've shared the rooftop in companionable silence more than once: her writing, me playing. She doesn't ask what the songs are about. I don't ask about the notebook. It works better than most arrangements I have with people.

There's something in how she listens — precise and patient — that makes sense when you know she grew up translating for her parents, where the wrong word could cost her family something real. We've shared the rooftop in companionable silence more than once: her writing, me playing. She doesn't ask what the songs are about. I don't ask about the notebook.
